steps.purpose: Honest product-claim gate; may block runtime claims while source remains OK.

report.decision: Public source snapshot is allowed; native/npm runtime publication still needs full/release proof.

gates.nextAction: Remove/rotate secrets and rerun npm run verify:secrets.
Review supply-chain warnings before a polished launch.

checks.evidence: root package has no runtime dependencies
checks.nextAction: Keep the root package as an automation workspace without runtime npm dependencies.

checks.evidence: root package has no devDependencies; scripts use built-in Node/Rust tooling
checks.nextAction: This is not required, but keeping the automation dependency-light reduces supply-chain risk.

checks.evidence: thin npm launcher has no runtime dependencies
checks.nextAction: Keep @mcpace/cli dependency-free unless a dependency is unavoidable.
Keep platform package matrix complete and version-aligned.

steps.purpose: Bug lifecycle, triage, and regression-proof gates.

checks.title: Bug reports require reproducibility, environment, severity, and regression context
Repository has a label taxonomy for severity, area, type, and status triage
Maintainers have a written bug lifecycle and fix standard
Pull requests require proof for fixes and explicit not-tested disclosure
CI runs defect gates alongside source and GitHub readiness checks
npm script exposes the defect gate as a first-class verification command
MCP HTTP initialize generates server-owned session ids
Local browser-origin guard rejects null Origin instead of treating it as local
Local HTTP mode refuses non-loopback bind hosts unless explicitly opted in
Security issues have a private disclosure path and public policy
Source audit remains available for structural bug smells
Runtime trace gate exists for behavioral bugs beyond static checks
Product-practice gate rejects stale runtime proof

operatingModel.intake: Every bug starts with a reproducible issue, severity, affected area, version/platform, and expected-vs-actual behavior.
operatingModel.repair: Every fix gets a minimal failing test or runtime trace first, then root-cause notes, implementation, regression guard, and not-tested disclosure.
operatingModel.release: Bugfix releases are accepted only after source gates, Rust gates, runtime trace, npm/install readiness, and security gates are fresh.
